message:
perf(storage): skip empty relation queries in get_issue_with_details

The hot detail-render path (br show, br info, br ready -v) called
six relation queries unconditionally per issue:

  - get_labels(id)
  - get_dependencies_with_metadata(id)
  - get_dependents_with_metadata(id)
  - get_comments(id)        (when include_comments)
  - get_parent_id(id)

Most issues in a real backlog have empty relation sets — no
labels, no comments, no parent — so each call paid the prepare /
bind / step / row-decode cost just to return an empty Vec.

Replace the unconditional fan-out with a single
issue_detail_relation_presence() probe that runs one
query_row_with_params:

  SELECT
    EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?),
    EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ?),
    EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM dependencies WHERE depends_on_id = ?),
    EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM comments WHERE issue_id = ?),
    (SELECT depends_on_id FROM dependencies
     WHERE issue_id = ? AND type = 'parent-child'
     ORDER BY rowid DESC LIMIT 1);

The four EXISTS clauses each touch one index entry at most. The
parent lookup folds get_parent_id() into the same row so we don't
need a second round-trip when the issue has a parent — and when it
has no parent the column is just NULL. The downstream get_*
calls now run only when the boolean is true; the comments path
still respects include_comments.

Result: an issue with no labels / deps / dependents / comments /
parent now costs one prepared SELECT instead of five (six with
include_comments). For issues with relations, the cost is one
extra cheap probe per detail render.

The IssueDetailRelationPresence struct is module-private and
non-Clone — it's a transient gate, not API surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
